description: UX improvements: Error Boundary, StyleQuiz fix to include custom designs, loading skeletons on Collections, chat history persistence in sessionStorage, WhatsApp URL encoding fix, enhanced Joy chatbot personality
steps: Create `src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx`: A React class component that catches render errors. Shows a friendly fallback UI with the brand styling (centered card, bronze accent, message like 'Something went wrong', a 'Refresh Page' button that calls window.location.reload()). Export it as default.
In `src/App.tsx`: Import ErrorBoundary and wrap the entire returned JSX (the root div) with <ErrorBoundary>. This catches any unhandled render errors across the app.

In `src/components/StyleQuiz.tsx`: The `recommend()` function currently only scores the imported static `designs`. Fix it to accept a `designs` parameter. The component should use allDesigns (custom + static). Import `useCustomDesigns` from context, merge with static designs using useMemo, and pass the merged array to recommend(). This ensures custom cloud designs are also scored by the quiz.

In `src/components/Collections.tsx`: Import `useCustomDesigns` to get the `loading` state. When loading is true, render 6 skeleton placeholder cards before the real grid. Each skeleton: a div with `animate-pulse` class, a gray rectangle for the image area (aspect-[4/5]), and two smaller gray bars for text. Show these instead of (or before) the real cards when loading.

In `src/components/Chatbot.tsx`: Persist chat state to sessionStorage. On mount, read geminiHistory from sessionStorage key 'joy-gemini-history' and messages from 'joy-messages'. After each message exchange (both user and bot messages added), save both to sessionStorage. This way chat survives page reloads within the same tab session.

In `src/utils/whatsapp.ts`: The `orderMessage()` function embeds designName and designId directly into the message string, and then `buildWhatsAppUrl()` encodes the whole message. However, verify that designName with special chars (emojis, &, #, etc.) is safe. The current pattern is already correct (buildWhatsAppUrl does encodeURIComponent on the full message). But add a safety check: in `orderMessage` and `styleConsultMessage`, sanitize the inputs by trimming them. Also ensure any other place that builds WhatsApp URLs (like in Chatbot or Lightbox) uses buildWhatsAppUrl properly.

Enhance Joy chatbot personality in `src/services/geminiChat.ts` buildSystemInstruction(): Add to the PERSONALITY section: 'Always complete your thoughts fully - never cut off mid-sentence. If recommending multiple options, present them clearly. Use warm Nigerian-friendly language occasionally (like ''This would look lovely on you, sis!''). When a customer seems undecided, offer to narrow it down with gentle questions about their budget, event date, or color preference. End messages with a helpful next step or question to keep the conversation flowing.'
acceptance_criteria: ErrorBoundary.tsx exists and is wrapped around the app content in App.tsx
StyleQuiz recommend() function scores both static and custom designs
Collections shows loading skeletons (animate-pulse divs) while cloud designs are loading
Chatbot reads/writes gemini history and messages to/from sessionStorage
WhatsApp URL building properly encodes all dynamic content
Joy chatbot system prompt includes enhanced personality traits
findings: All changes compile and build cleanly. ErrorBoundary wraps App root. StyleQuiz now scores both custom and static designs using allDesigns merged via useMemo. Collections shows loading skeletons while custom designs load and hides the real grid until loading completes. Chatbot persists geminiHistory and messages to sessionStorage. WhatsApp inputs are trimmed. Joy personality enhanced with warm Nigerian-friendly expressions and conversational guidance.
